content:
LEGAL NAMEArvid Basalis
CODENAMEThe Mantis
DATE OF BIRTH1952/09/23
AGE38 (estimated)
GENDERTransmasc; he/him
SECURITY CLEARANCELevel-Psi "Apeiros"
RANKDeputy Director
DIVISIONVermilion Network
DATE OF ARRIVAL1991/11/24
AFFILIATIONPandora's Acolytes LLC
LOCATIONUnknown
STATUSAlive
// PHYSIOLOGY

Basalis is a bipedal invertebrate of unknown species (though it has been named SCP-8856 by the SCP Foundation) standing at 144.78 centimeters and weighing 32.6 kilograms. Limited analysis has noted a layered exoskeleton consisting of both hard chitinous plates and a softer, more flexible plating on the outer layer; the inner layer is entirely hard, segmented chitin serving a similar purpose to a skeleton in a vertebrate. Basalis is heavily dependent on his antennae for vision, being able to detect even the finest movements within a 15 meter radius and larger vibrations and some speech at upwards of 100 meters. His mandibles are fully independent and able to latch on to objects, but lack an opposable joint required for more acute grips.

// MEDICAL HISTORY

Basalis possesses a severe form of true polycoria, having two additional pupils in his right eye and one in his left. Because of this, his vision is limited; recent tests have shown vision at 20/300. Additionally, two previous deaths, both due to gunshot wounds to the head, have left residual damage despite successful attempts at revival. This has led to frequent disassociation and migraines, as well as a noticeable increase in hostility.

// AUGMENTS

Following two resurrections, both the result of executions carried out by the now defunct Vanguard Corporation, Basalis was outfitted with low profile cerebral augments that allow for a direct connection to VEDbC.net. With this, he is able to access the database at will, override and directly view nearby camera systems, and disrupt nearby electrical signals. Overuse of this can worsen already present migraines. Additionally, when connected to VEDbC.net or foreign systems, any damage to those systems is physically felt by Basalis. As of XXXX/02/19, Basalis has gained DATA EXPUNGED BY ACOLYTES DIRECTORSHIP.

// INVENTORY
PRIMARYAUG A3
SIDEARMGlock-17
MELEECombat Knife
MELEEWar Sickle x2. A pair of worn, black steel sickles with handles wrapped in tattered cloth. Origin unknown.
SPECIALNanobots gifted by O5-VIII "Thunder," stored around Mantis' lower pair of forearms. He has full control over their movement; said nanobots can be used offensively or defensively.
// TRINKETS
BLOODIED GOGGLESA pair of glowing yellow goggles with cracked lenses and blood stains. They always hang from Mantis' belt, but he refuses to talk about them.
METAL FLASKA small but intricate metal flask. Suspected to have previously belonged to one of the former O5-IXs.
THORN RINGKept on a string around Mantis' neck. It seems to radiate an unknown kind of energy; whatever it is, it feels vengeful.
// FOUNDATION HISTORY

See Foundation transcripts SCP-8856 and H-1907.1 Arvid Basalis for further detail.

Basalis was initially captured on 1959/05/03 after receiving several reports of missing persons in the vicinity of REDACTED, Oregon, USA. Gamma-4 "Green Stags" was dispatched, and despite suffering heavy losses, was able to restrain and tranquilize Basalis. He was then transferred to Site-44. In 1973, Basalis began working with Alpha-9 "Last Hope," being utilized to assist with reconnaissance and containment requiring more aggressive methods. This lasted until 1976/07/28, when he was approved to become Foundation staff as a member of the Intelligence Agency. He specialized in handling Hostile GoI, including the elimination of members within such. His deployments were kept limited, with only about 4 total operations. Additionally, a killswitch was installed in the base of his neck to prevent any attempts at going rogue. This led to a transfer to ISD in 1981 under the Reduviidae Division, and an eventual nomination to O5-II-A in 1986 and later O5-II in 1989.

Basalis remained within his position until 1981/09/23, when he was moved to the Internal Security Department. Though Basalis himself was not deployed often, he was considered to be relatively efficient when he was on the field. More often than not, he dealt with high-risk or volatile situations that could lead to the collapse of a site if handled improperly. Unlike most Reduviidae Division operatives, he did not continue work as an ISD field agent while off-duty. Instead, he was often interviewed by high-ranking members of the Scientific Department, primarily with the goal to gain a further understanding of him and his species. So far, interviews have been inconclusive.

After REDACTED, Basalis was nominated as a candidate for O5-II-F on 1986/04/11. After an 8-5 vote, he was initiated under the codename "The Mantis." He maintained this position until 1989/02/20 after the retirement of the previous O5-II. Basalis was subsequently promoted and would remain O5-II until Protocol Arcturus.

// ARCTURUS PROTOCOL

Basalis' hostility towards the Foundation was first noted around 1990/01/22 with the murder of O5-X. However, some records show hostile activity before this. Though most information has been purged from the Foundation's database, camera systems in Site-73 recorded Basalis making a deal with the commander of MTF Epsilon-1 on 1990/01/14. All information regarding Epsilon-1 was purged before access could be gained to it, but it is suspected that they were initially involved in what is referred to as "Arcturus Protocol."

On 1990/01/22, Basalis and his factotum, codenamed DATA EXPUNGED BY REQUEST OF THE ETHICS COMMITTEE. ANY ATTEMPTS TO RECOVER REDACTED INFORMATION WITHOUT ADEQUATE CLEARANCE WILL RESULT IN IMMEDIATE TERMINATION. He was tried by the Ethics Committee for the murder of O5-X, resulting in a three (3) month suspension of duties. Basalis made no effort to hire another factotum.

[SUPPRESSED INTERNAL RECORD]

now, why did you do what you did?
that's his blood on your hands.
no, mine. yours are mine and mine are yours.
we killed him. not the red right hand,
not the committee, no. that was us.

After a series of incidents in which Basalis was noted to express increased levels of hostility toward certain high ranking individuals within the Foundation, monitoring was increased. Despite this, it was later discovered that between 1990/03/07 and 1990/09/02, Basalis was engaged in over 50 meetings and operations unassociated with, and considered treasonous towards, the Foundation itself. The epitome of this was 1990/09/04, in which an event designated "Protocol Arcturus" was initiated.

Under Basalis' command, insurgents from task forces Alpha-1 "Red Right Hand" and Omega-1 "Law's Left Hand", as well as members of the Internal Security Department, overtook several Foundation sites, including Site-14 "Winter", Site-17 "Deepwell", Site-44 "Verdant Archives", Site-73 "Recon", and Site-98 "Abyss". The detonation of both the Alpha and Omega warheads at each site was attempted. This only proved to be successful at Site-14 and Site-73; all other sites involved were able to disable the warheads before detonation.

During the incident, Basalis and the insurgents under him attempted to flee Site-98 to what is now suspected to be former Protected Area-16 "Arcturus". Though most escaped, Basalis was tranquilized before reaching the site exit. He was held in Site-98's Sector-R until 1990/09/13, when the site was destroyed by order of Foundation High Command.

[SUPPRESSED INTERNAL RECORD]

you failed them.

Basalis' rank was stripped, and he was transported back to Site-44 as an SCP after heavy amnesticization. He remained there until 1991/11/24, when members of Pandora's Acolytes LLC raided the site. Though not confirmed, it is suspected that Basalis willingly allowed himself to be captured, and left Site-44 with Pandora's Acolytes.

[SUPPRESSED INTERNAL RECORD]

you certainly aren't free as much as you'd like to pretend you are.
it is a cycle of death you will never escape.

// SCP-8856 FOUNDATION FILE
ITEM NUMBER8856
CLEARANCELevel 5 (Top Secret)
CONTAINMENTEuclid-Thaumiel
DISRUPTIONKeneq (3)
RISKDanger (4)

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-8856 is to be kept in a 15 m x 15 m x 7 m containment chamber within Site-44. The containment chamber is to be kept well ventilated and filled with foliage similar to that of the rainforests within the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. Once per day, one (1) Class-D personnel is to be allowed into SCP-8856's containment chamber for the purpose of allowing the SCP to hunt. If SCP-8856 shows signs of increased hostility, it is to be tranquilized and kept under anesthesia for 24 hours.

Description: SCP-8856 appears to be a short, humanoid male, standing at about 145 cm. It is suspected to be part of a larger species with behaviors similar to that of the order Mantodea. SCP-8856 displays several insectoid features, including antennae, prominent mandibles, an extra pair of arms, and wings that are capable of folding under chitinous plates. Though suspected to have poor eyesight due to what may be a form of polycoria, it is capable of detecting movement with its antennae within a 15 meter radius.

SCP-8856 has shown to possess human levels of sapience, capable of language comprehension and speech. It is considered to be aggressive, though often enters periods of time in which it is docile until threatened. While in a hostile state, SCP-8856 experiences what is described as an "inexplicable hunger." It displays cannibalistic tendencies, preferring to hunt in a method similar to known ambush predators such as mantids and leopards. Its speech capabilities tend to reduce to communicating through a series of chitters and chirps; however, it can still understand speech while in this state.

SCP-8856 was recovered on 1993/05/03 in the forests surrounding Mt. Hood, Oregon, North America after several reports of missing persons and remains found in the area. A team from MTF Gamma-4 "Green Stags" was dispatched, where they subsequently discovered SCP-8856. It was successfully tranquilized after the team suffered heavy losses, likely due to underestimating SCP-8856's capabilities. Reports relay that Gamma-4 had "suspected [they] were being followed for about 2 kilometres," then were "ambushed from the left flank." SCP-8856 was temporarily held at Area-REDACTED until it was transferred to Site-44 on 1993/05/12.

[LOADING ATTACHED FILE. . .] [TRANSCRIPT 1973/04/28]

TO: judaswalton@scip.net
FROM: [DATA RESTRICTED]
SUBJECT: SCP-8856

Walton, SCP-8856 has been cleared as an asset for Alpha-9 "Last Hope." It is not to be left unsupervised when outside of his containment chamber, and will be provided an explosive collar that is programmed to detonate in the event it attempts to escape. Additionally, it will be given an embedded killswitch and tracker, assuming Orihon budges. Should be set by May 1st. Your recent recommendations and dedication have been noted. If this goes well, I'll be putting in a good word with your Director. Competence doesn't go unnoticed in the Foundation. C.R. Galanis

[END OF FILE.]

[SUPPRESSED INTERNAL RECORD]

the ones above have left you behind, and you know that.

// QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS
Q: Why did the Foundation keep him alive for so long?
In short, he was still useful. Keep in mind the Foundation tends to operate off of whether something is useful and whether they can actually do something to it if it gets out of control. Until Mantis fully went rogue, he was of use getting the Foundation information they couldn't normally obtain, and getting rid of individuals the Foundation would normally struggle to eliminate. Even as an SCP, he still held use in that he was the only member of his species they had located so far. Therefore, they needed him for information regarding SCP-8856 instances. The only reason they bothered keeping him alive is they knew (or thought) they could keep him under wraps. They knew he could still be amnesticized. They thought they could just set off his killswitch if they needed to. As far as they knew, they were entirely capable of controlling Mantis when they needed to, so it would have been a waste to kill them.
Q: Why did Mantis turn on the Foundation?
Simply put, paranoia spiral. This is only well-known if you were around Mantis in Site-73, but he originally was suspicious of other members of the Council because of talk of turning on each other and the Foundation. That O5 he killed? GOC double agent. As he realized the Foundation (especially the Ethics Committee) was preventing him from doing his job, he started to believe that the Foundation was corrupt in of itself. This led to him jumping to more and more extreme methods the more backlash he got from the Foundation. It didn't help that his Factotum was killed by Alpha-1 and the factotum-turned-O5 he unofficially adopted was brainwashed by O5-1, but that's another thing entirely.
Q: Why don't they just raid Area-16?
Mantis, alongside other former Internal Security agents, would likely have made sure that information about Area-16 is as restricted as possible, due to the sensitive nature of the department's operations. Though not specified in the secondary file (as I have it written for L4 non-ISD clearance, not L5), Area-16 is my headcanon for the Internal Security Department's headquarters. ISD could have easily purged a majority of information on Area-16 before the incident and made it certain that any attempts to reveal information surrounding Area-16 would result in a major data leak. Plus, if Mantis is willing to take down multiple sites at once, who says he won't do the same to his own HQ if given a reason to?
Q: Is Mantis a cannibal?
Short answer, yes. I have chosen to refrain from acknowledging this part of his lore while on-site for the sake of following TOS so I don't get smited by the moderation team.
Q: who the fuck is walton
it's walton bruh

Last updated 2026/03/02.
